title: Golden Crunchy Irish Beef Shin Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful Irish stew made with beef shin, nuka-pickle, and crunchy elements, perfect for a weeknight dinner, this dish is also vegan-friendly when using vegan Worcestershire sauce and vegan-friendly nuka-pickle.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat.
Add the beef shin pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the browned beef shin from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it starts to caramelize, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes to the pot and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the nuka-pickle, Worcestershire sauce, tomato paste, and vegetable broth to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the browned beef shin to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven, cooking for 2.5 hours.
While the stew is cooking, mix the oats and nutritional yeast in a small bowl.
After 2 hours of cooking, stir in the oat and nutritional yeast mixture to add a crunchy element to the stew.
Refrigerate and consume within 1 week — this preparation is not shelf-stable without an approved acid level and canning process.

Refrigerator pickle only: store in the fridge and consume within 1 week. For shelf-stable canning, add at least 2 tablespoons of vinegar (5% acidity) per cup of brine and follow a tested water-bath process.
